L2.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine where internal parse-time optimization data structures are incorrectly referenced during function parameter list processing. When crafted JavaScript containing nested constructs such as arrow functions or function expressions within formal parameters is parsed, this leads to out-of-bounds memory access and potential remote code execution.

L3.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine where internal parse-time optimization data structures are incorrectly referenced during function parameter list processing. When crafted JavaScript containing nested constructs such as arrow functions or function expressions within formal parameters is parsed, this leads to out-of-bounds memory access and potential remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The parser incorrectly uses deferred function stub data while processing formal argument lists, causing out-of-bounds memory access when arrow functions or nested functions appear within function parameters.
